steps.title: Apply the zero-product property
steps.content: The equation is already factored, so set each factor of $(3x+8)(4x-3)=0$ to zero: $3x+8=0$ gives $x=-\tfrac{8}{3}$, and $4x-3=0$ gives $x=\tfrac{3}{4}$.

steps.title: Identify the matching solution
steps.content: The solutions are $-\tfrac{8}{3}$ and $\tfrac{3}{4}$. That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: As a final check, compare the result from the work to the answer choices. The value, equation, or condition found above matches choice A, $- \frac{8}{3}$, and the other choices fail the same setup or substitution. Therefore A is correct.
